summary: The command scaffolding is mostly solid, but it never rejects nonexistent <cwd> paths, so the feature misses its assigned VAL-CMD-017 behavior and lacks test coverage for that case.

issues.description: The command accepts nonexistent cwd values and forwards them to the bridge instead of returning the required not-found error.
issues.evidence: In packages/webapp/src/shell/supplemental-commands/agent-command.ts, resolveCwd() only normalizes the string path, and createAgentCommand() immediately uses resolvedCwd and then calls bridge.spawn() without any ctx.fs.exists()/ctx.fs.stat() validation (see the resolveCwd() helper and the resolvedCwd/bridge.spawn flow around lines 139-233). The cwd tests in packages/webapp/tests/shell/supplemental-commands/agent-command.test.ts cover relative, '.', '..', absolute, and empty-string cwd cases, but there is no nonexistent-path case even though this feature fulfills VAL-CMD-017 in mission features.json. The handoff also overstates coverage by claiming all CMD-* assertions are covered.
issues.suggestedFix: Validate the resolved cwd before bridge lookup/spawn (for example with ctx.fs.stat(resolvedCwd) and an isDirectory check), return a descriptive stderr/non-zero exit when the path is missing or invalid, and add a focused test for `agent /does/not/exist "*" "p"`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `.factory/library/environment.md` still tells workers to attach CDP on fixed port 9222, but the mission AGENTS.md and .factory/services.yaml already document that Chrome may auto-allocate another port and workers should prefer ws://localhost:5710/cdp or discover the port from the dev-server log.
sharedStateObservations.why: This stale library guidance can send future workers and validators to the wrong browser target during manual verification.
